findings.title: PROP-065 authored — workspace-sync universal-pusher destructive overwrite of .jsonl files (DIRECTIVE-20260531-001 directive)
findings.diagnosis_summary: Universal-pusher's smart_copy uses mtime + content-cmp + PROP-045 anti-reversion as defenses for FUSE→git pushes. For .jsonl files written exclusively via clone-and-push (producer-on-clone-pushes pattern), the FUSE replica is briefly stale between push and git→FUSE propagation. Any FUSE-mtime bump in that window triggers smart_copy to push FUSE-state back, deleting the new row. Anti-reversion SHOULD catch this (FUSE content matches historic git commit) but proved LEAKY: FND-02 event's FUSE sha1 e49c38f1... was byte-identical to commit 6be7216 yet anti-reversion did not fire. Root cause undiagnosed (clone depth edge case, bash variable lifetime, or anti-reversion bug — tracked as PROP-065 follow-up). Fix: deny-list, not backstop.
findings.recommendation: Apply PROP-065 patches: ~30 lines to monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md, ~15 lines to build.js OWNERSHIP, ~20 lines to CLAUDE.md File Ownership Rules. Single commit. After deploy, tinker Mode 3 verifies via verification_pattern.
findings.blast_radius: ~65 lines patch surface; behavior change limited to 15 .jsonl paths (FUSE→git push now refused); producer write paths unchanged; new skip-log reason code added (no schema break).
